theory.intro: A composite figure is made from simpler shapes. Split an L-shaped figure into rectangles, find each area, then add (or subtract a missing corner from a large rectangle).
facts: Area of a rectangle $=$ length $\times$ width.
**Split** the figure into rectangles and **add** the parts, or take a large rectangle and **subtract** the missing piece.
Find any missing side length using the other lengths (opposite sides of the whole add up).
steps: Cut the L-shape into two (or more) rectangles.
Work out any missing side lengths from the given ones.
Find each rectangle's area and add them together.
